reviewSummary.summary: The draft artifact is heavily polluted with upstream home_page controls, weak technical naming, duplicate navigation ownership, and missing core login workflow elements such as username/password fields, login submission control, and failed authentication validation elements.

issues.message: The Login page model is missing the core Username field required for authentication workflows.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a semantically named username input element with a stable locator such as an id, formcontrolname, or reliable placeholder-based locator.

issues.message: The Login page model is missing the Password field required for authentication and password masking validation.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a semantically named password input element with a stable locator and preserve support for masking validation.

issues.message: The Login page model is missing the Login button required for credential submission and authentication validation.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a semantically named login button element with a stable locator.

issues.message: The workflow requires explicit validation of the generic 'Failed' authentication message, but no failed authentication message element exists in the page model.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a reusable failed_authentication_message element with a stable locator suitable for negative authentication assertions.

issues.message: Customer signup button belongs to upstream home_page navigation and is not part of the Login page workflow ownership.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this element from the login_page artifact and reuse ownership from home_page.resource.

issues.message: Clean survey button belongs to upstream home_page navigation and is not part of the Login page workflow ownership.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this element from the login_page artifact and reuse ownership from home_page.resource.

issues.message: Site survey button belongs to upstream home_page navigation and is not part of the Login page workflow ownership.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this element from the login_page artifact and reuse ownership from home_page.resource.

issues.message: The element name is implementation-centric and not business-readable.
The element name is implementation-centric and not business-readable.
The element name is implementation-centric and not business-readable.
The element name is implementation-centric and not business-readable.
This generic anchor element duplicates the Home navigation control already represented by the home button element.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the duplicate generic anchor element and keep only the semantically meaningful home button control.

issues.message: Locator xpath=//a is dangerously generic and likely to match multiple links, creating brittle automation behavior.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this locator entirely or replace it with a uniquely attributable locator tied to the intended navigation control.

issues.message: Background content container is decorative/layout-only and provides no meaningful automation value.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the background content element from the approved page model.

issues.message: The underlying shadow-native control is an anchor/link but is classified only as a button.
issues.suggested_fix: Classify consistently as a navigation button or navigation link based on framework standards and intended reuse.

issues.message: The workflow explicitly requires authenticated-state validation after successful login, but no authenticated user identity display or authenticated-only feature indicator is modeled.
issues.suggested_fix: Add reusable authenticated state indicators owned by the appropriate upstream page resource.

approved_elements.name: person_profile_button
approved_elements.description: Opens the Login page from home_page guest state.

approved_elements.name: back_button
approved_elements.description: Returns the user to home_page without authentication.

approved_elements.name: home_button
approved_elements.description: Returns the user to home_page without authentication.
